summary: Tom presented the revised proposal to Lisa. Lisa agreed to the proposed timeline and confirmed she would proceed with the contract by the end of the month. She had some questions about the integration with their existing TMS but those were resolved during the call. Next step: contract signature next week.

transcript.speaker: rep
transcript.name: Tom
transcript.text: Lisa, thanks for the time. Did you get a chance to look at the revised proposal?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.name: Lisa
transcript.text: Ja, ich habe es zweimal gelesen — once on the train Monday and again last night.

transcript.speaker: rep
transcript.name: Tom
transcript.text: Goed. My system says 'Offline' so I assumed you hadn't seen it.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.name: Lisa
transcript.text: Phone reading I think. Anyway — I have some questions.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.name: Lisa
transcript.text: The TMS integration — how long does the data migration take? We have five years of records.

transcript.speaker: rep
transcript.name: Tom
transcript.text: Typically four to six weeks for that volume. We can phase it if you want to be operational on the new platform sooner.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.name: Lisa
transcript.text: Hmm. Let me think about that with my team. The phasing might be necessary.

transcript.speaker: rep
transcript.name: Tom
transcript.text: Sure. What's your timeline thinking — end of month for a decision?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.name: Lisa
transcript.text: Maybe. I want to discuss with our COO first. He's traveling but back next week.

transcript.speaker: rep
transcript.name: Tom
transcript.text: Understood. Should I plan a follow-up call once you've spoken with him?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.name: Lisa
transcript.text: Ja, please. I'll let you know when.
